title: rubus trachycaulon
intro: Rubus trachycaulon is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), commonly known as a type of blackberry or bramble. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and wounds, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The plant's leaves, stems, and fruits have been used in herbal preparations to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Rubus trachycaulon can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
